rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings like assignments
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid scopes using visible feature names
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope for the contract
Use visible identifiers to reference features in contracts
Avoid mixing component type and implementation contracts unless necessary
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Relate outputs to inputs in output dependency contracts
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not place assumptions and guarantees in invalid scopes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax notes for assume/guarantee syntax
Defensive rules about AADL property names and AGREE variables
Pattern library examples for type scope range assumption and guarantee
Pattern library examples for type scope deterministic dependency guarantee
